deliverables.summary: Implementation remains byte-unchanged because the packet's mandatory actual-Windows runtime proof is unavailable at preflight.

decisions.summary: Stop before behavior-changing edits because the mandatory actual Windows MSVC runtime proof is unavailable.
decisions.authority_effect: The approved packet explicitly states that macOS/Linux execution or Windows cross-compilation cannot substitute for the two named Windows runtime tests. Beginning implementation without an accessible Windows host would knowingly create an uncloseable slice and violate the frozen stop condition.

findings.summary: The current host is macOS arm64; the repository CI matrix contains only Ubuntu and macOS; the repository has zero configured Actions runners; no organization Windows runner was accessible. Therefore the two mandatory runtime tests cannot execute on an actual Windows MSVC host in this run.

blockers.summary: No accessible actual Windows MSVC execution host or runner exists for the packet's two mandatory named runtime tests.
blockers.resume_condition: Provide access to an actual Windows MSVC host or CI runner capable of checking out the exact HCM-1.4 implementation subject and executing both named cargo test commands from the SPEC with captured host OS/architecture, Rust target, command, exit status, and test name.

resume.objective: Resume the exact HCM-1.4 implementation packet from pre-edit preflight, execute it test-first, prove it on an actual Windows MSVC host, obtain fresh clean review, and complete the two-commit closeout without starting HCM-2.
resume.condition: An actual Windows MSVC host or runner is accessible to the top-level orchestrator for both mandatory named runtime tests, with an exact mechanism to test the final uncommitted or review-bound HCM-1.4 subject without changing the packet's forbidden CI/Cargo scope.

sections: HCM-1.4 profile-aware setup and doctor decision proof gate

allowed_scope: Use the exact existing HCM-1.4 SPEC.md, tasks/plan.md, and tasks/todo.md packet without reopening reviewed planning bytes.
Edit only the exact engine, compiler, CLI, test, control-pack, proof, dispatch, and closeout paths allowlisted by the HCM-1.4 SPEC.
Use the available actual Windows MSVC execution mechanism only for the two required named runtime tests and already-authorized cross-platform proof.
prohibited_scope: Do not begin implementation until the actual Windows runtime execution path is available.

Do not add or modify CI, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, definition assets, schemas, condition evidence/evaluator contracts, canonical writes, compatibility bridges, new CLI grammar, compiler publication, or HCM-2 work merely to manufacture platform proof.
Do not treat cross-compilation, Wine, macOS, Linux, or an emulated compile-only check as actual Windows runtime evidence.
Do not modify or supersede the approved planning bytes without new evidence and explicit planning authority.
acceptance_criteria: Preflight identifies an accessible actual Windows MSVC host/runner and records how the final exact subject will be exercised without forbidden-scope changes.
Both named Windows runtime tests execute on that host and their host/target/command/status/test-name evidence is included in the final proof wall.
Every remaining HCM-1.4 implementation, regression, package, scope, security, fresh-review, and two-commit closeout gate passes exactly as frozen.
stop_conditions: Stop again with external_blocker if the actual Windows runtime execution path is still unavailable or cannot exercise the exact final subject.
Stop if satisfying the platform gate would require a file or authority outside the approved HCM-1.4 packet.
Stop on any other frozen HCM-1.4 stop condition rather than widening scope.

chat_summary: HCM-1.4 stopped during clean pre-edit preflight. The approved packet requires two named runtime tests on an actual Windows MSVC host and explicitly rejects cross-compilation as a substitute; this macOS checkout has no accessible Windows runner. Resume only after such a host is available without widening the frozen packet.
